pattern_recognition: Greedy Algorithm; the problem can be categorized under dynamic programming and greedy strategies since we need to make optimal choices at each step to minimize jumps.

approaches.title: Brute Force
approaches.intuition: Try all possible jumps from each position and find the minimum jumps needed to reach the last index.
approaches.algorithm: Recursively explore all jump possibilities, keeping track of the number of jumps taken.

approaches.title: Optimal Approach
approaches.intuition: Use a greedy strategy to keep track of the farthest point reachable with the current number of jumps, minimizing the total jumps needed.
approaches.algorithm: Iterate through the array, updating the farthest reachable index and counting jumps when the current index reaches the end of the current jump range.

dry_run: Consider the input array [2, 3, 1, 1, 4]. Start at index 0, with a jump of 2. From index 0, we can jump to index 1 or 2. Jumping to index 1 (value 3), we can reach index 4 directly. Thus, we take 2 jumps to reach the end.
edge_cases: An empty input array should return 0 jumps. A single element array should also return 0 jumps. Duplicates in the array should not affect the result. Large inputs should be handled efficiently without exceeding time limits.
common_pitfalls: 1) Assuming you can jump to any index without considering the jump limits; correct is to only jump within the limits. 2) Not updating the farthest reachable index correctly; correct is to always check if the current index can extend the reach. 3) Failing to count jumps accurately when reaching the end of the current jump range; correct is to increment the jump count at the right moments.
